contract.purpose: Architecturally significant decision records — one decision each, with rationale and the rejected alternative; the top authority tier, file-per-decision, append-only.

policy.purpose: Standing rules — ongoing directives that govern behavior across sessions; the second authority tier.

schema.purpose: Structural contracts — JSON Schema (2020-12) declaring the shape of structured files and of prose frontmatter.

check.purpose: Declarative validation rules the validator dispatches (target, kind, params, tier, suites, message) — authored as data, never as validator code.

tool.purpose: The engine's executable machinery — the validator, hooks, MCP servers, the wiring library, and interface implementations.

operation.purpose: The authoritative steps of a multi-step engine procedure performed by reading-and-following; one procedure, one home, referenced by its invokers rather than restated.

skill.purpose: In-session procedures (Claude Code SKILL.md, progressive disclosure), engine-prefixed; invoked per the model-auto / operator-typed / model-only axis.

agent.purpose: Personas the engine runs for a trigger (review, worker, and audit roles), routed by role, lens, model tier, permissions, and output contract.

interface.purpose: Protocol contracts — a stable callable boundary a swappable implementation satisfies; implementations bind by presence, resolve single-active, and name a fallback.

doc.purpose: Operator-facing, hand-authored plain-language explanations of the engine — written for the human, not the AI.

state.purpose: The project's committed cursor — the small pointers-and-counts file that answers 'where does the work stand?' at the start of every session, and stays readable when online services are down.
